name: VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East
name_original: Condom et le lac de Bousquétara — boucle
description: Hike the VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East, a moderate 10.1-mile trail with 648 feet of gain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Condom, located in the Gers department of Gascogne, traverse a landscape characterized by rolling agricultural fields, vineyards, and river valleys. The region features gentle hills and historic villages, offering accessible routes for various fitness levels. The terrain typically consists of well-maintained paths and quiet country roads, suitable for relaxed exploration. Best easy hiking trails around Condom The most popular easy hiking route is View of La Romieu – La Romieu loop from La Romieu, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete, offering views of the historic village of La Romieu. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lake of the Three Valleys loop from Cloutats, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This route features scenic stretches around the lake, providing a tranquil natural setting. Local hikers also love the Larressingle Fortified Village – Larresingle, medieval village loop from Larressingle, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through a historic fortified village, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Hiking around Condom is defined by rolling hills, agricultural landscapes, and historic villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, focusing on easy hiking trails. The routes in Condom are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Condom's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are there around Condom?
A: There are over 80 easy hiking trails available around Condom, offering a variety of routes through the region's gentle hills, agricultural fields, and historic villages. In total, komoot lists over 250 hiking routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Condom?
A: The easy hiking trails around Condom typically feature rolling agricultural fields, vineyards, and river valleys. The terrain consists mostly of well-maintained paths and quiet country roads, making them accessible for various fitness levels and suitable for relaxed exploration.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks in the Condom area?
A: Yes, many easy routes in Condom are circular. For example, you can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20638728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from La Romieu&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 5.9 km long, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont d'Artigues – Armagnac Greenway loop from Le Mirail&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 6.9 km.

Q: What interesting historical sites or natural features can I see on these easy hikes?
A: Many easy hikes in Condom pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3770084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larressingle Fortified Village&lt;/a&gt; on a loop trail, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3903609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Peter's Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; in Condom itself. For natural beauty, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15105662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys loop from Cloutats&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic stretches around the lake.

Q: Are the easy trails around Condom suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the easy trails in Condom are generally well-suited for families due to their gentle terrain and well-maintained paths. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10609610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Peter's Cathedral – Condom loop from Condom&lt;/a&gt; are shorter and offer points of interest within the town, making them ideal for walks with children.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Condom?
A: The easy hiking trails around Condom are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil natural settings, the charm of the historic villages, and the accessibility of the routes.

Q: Is there parking available near the easy hiking trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many of the easy hiking routes in the Condom area start from or pass through villages and towns where public parking is typically available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Condom?
A: The Condom region, like much of Gascony, experiences pleasant weather for hiking from spring through autumn. Spring offers blooming landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during peak season.

Q: Are there any easy hikes that pass by lakes or water features?
A: Yes, you can find easy hikes that feature water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15105662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys loop from Cloutats&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering tranquil views and scenic stretches around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3201029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys&lt;/a&gt;. Another nearby lake is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5812764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bousquétara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find refreshments or cafes near the easy hiking routes?
A: Many easy hiking routes in Condom either start from or pass through charming villages and towns where you can find cafes, bakeries, and restaurants. For example, routes around La Romieu or Larressingle offer opportunities to stop for refreshments in these historic settings.

Q: Are there any specific villages or towns worth exploring on an easy hike?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with picturesque villages. The easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10609619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Romieu – La Romieu loop from La Romieu&lt;/a&gt; allows you to explore the historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4397306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Romieu&lt;/a&gt;. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larressingle Fortified Village – Larresingle, medieval village loop from Larressingle&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through one of France's 'most beautiful villages'.
tips: Gîte de Gabarre is a former Armagnac winery renovated with local limestone. It is part of a series of buildings built in the 19th century that allowed the production and packaging in barrels of distilled brandy.

Condom Cathedral is a Catholic church and former cathedral dedicated to Saint Peter in Condom, Gers, France. It was classified as a historic monument in 1840. It was formerly the seat of the bishops of Condom; the diocese was added to the Archdiocese of Auch in 1822.

This cathedral, former diocese of Condom between 1317 and 1790, has been classified as a historic monument since 1840. We can easily see the scars of the wars of religion on its facades as well as in the heart of its magnificent cloister with Gothic vaults. Since 2010, the statue of the Dumas musketeers has stood on the cathedral square. It was created by the Georgian sculptor Zurab Tseretel.
Het meer van Bousquetara ligt tussen Condom in Castelnau-sur-l'Auvignon, in het departement Gers (32), in de regio Occitanie.

faqs.question: Is VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East suitable for hiking?
faqs.answer: Yes, while designated as a VTT (mountain biking) circuit, its relatively low elevation difference makes it well-suited for hiking. It's generally considered an easy route, accessible to most hikers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circuit?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, featuring both stony and sandy sections. You'll encounter typical local flora such as box trees, oaks, blackthorns, and thistles. The path offers a blend of natural landscapes and historical elements.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The circuit offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical elements. You can see the deserted farm 'Camp Biel' with its magnificent vaulted U-shaped building, ancient dolmens near Sayssou, and observe well-preserved agropastoral heritage like jasses and lavogne. You'll also pass by Saint Peter's Cathedral and the Statue of the Three Musketeers at Saint-Pierre Cathedral, Condom, and skirt Lake Bousquétara.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers splendid 360-degree views, particularly over the Verzolet valley. In the distance, you can also catch a glimpse of the impressive Château de Montalègre beyond the Sorgues valley.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East?
faqs.answer: As this is a loop trail starting and ending near Condom, parking is typically available in or around the town. Specific trailhead parking details may vary, but you can usually find suitable spots in municipal parking areas.

faqs.question: Is the VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. We recommend keeping your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and always remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circuit?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking this circuit, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for cooler conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Circuit VTT n°5 and Circuit VTT n°6. You'll also find smaller overlaps with the Sentier de la Baïse and other local VTT circuits.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the VTT Circuit No. 5: The Rush to the East?
faqs.answer: The circuit is approximately 16.25 km long and typically takes around 4 hours and 21 minutes to complete at a moderate hiking pace. This duration can vary based on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the views or explore points of interest.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Condom, where you'll find various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Along the route, you might also encounter places like Gîte de Gabarre, which could offer services depending on the season.

waypoints.name: Statue of the Three Musketeers at Saint-Pierre Cathedral, Condom
